rulings.Case Title: RONNIE CAMERON VS. C.C. MOORE & CO. ENGINEERS
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Compel Further Responses To Discovery
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, May 1, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.¿s motion to compel further responses to discovery is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Compel Responses To General Order 129 Standard Interrogatories From Omega Products Corp.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, May 1, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel responses to General Order 129 standard plaintiff interrogatories from Omega Products Corp. and request for sanctions: The motion to compel is off calendar at plaintiffs¿ request since defendant has served verified responses. Plaintiffs¿ request for sanctions in the amount of $1061.00 is granted. Defendant failed to demonstrate substantial justification why it failed to timely comply with General Order 129 and why it failed to respond to plaintiffs¿ meet and confer letters. Defendant neither served responses nor responded to plaintiffs until plaintiffs had already filed this motion. Plaintiffs¿ request that hearings on any dispositive motions filed by defendant in asbestos cases before this court be stayed until 60 days after the date defendant served its verified responses is granted. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
